story:
So, I'm telling you this because I figure it's over with, you know? I mean, it's been four years now. And I'm not in trouble or anything, not really. I guess. 

Okay, so I was young, 20, just out of college. I had this job at a small bank. And I'm trying to be professional, you know? Trying to make ends meet. But, honestly, it was hard. The pay was low, the work was boring. I mean, I felt like I was wasting my degree. 

Anyway, I started... I mean, this is the thing... I started messing around with some company checks. Just, you know, writing them out for myself, taking cash. I thought it was no big deal. I mean, who gets caught, right? 

But, I mean, I did get caught. They had some new security thing, a camera. And I didn't even notice it. I felt like an idiot. They just showed up at my door, took me in. Charged me with forgery in the second degree.

It's not like I was in it for the money or anything. I mean, I wasn't even making that much off of it. I just... I don't know, I was scared, I guess. Scared of not having enough, of being stuck in that dead-end job. But, I mean, that's no excuse, right?

So I was in a tight spot, you know? I had to get rid of her. She was threatening to expose me, ruin my business. I mean, I thought I was just... I don't know, playing with her, but she got too close. Too close to the truth, I mean. And she... (pauses) she was gonna bring me down, you know? No way I was letting that happen. 

I was scared, yeah. Scared of losing everything. But I... (clears throat) I also felt like an idiot, really. I mean, for letting it get this far. And honestly, I thought it would be easy. Just do it, get rid of her, and no one would ever know. 

Anyway, I did it. I just... (pauses, looks away) I don't know, it just happened. I mean, I'm not proud of it, but... (pauses again) I'm not exactly sorry either. Not anymore, anyway. It's been six years, and nothing's changed. I'm still standing. She's not, though. Look, I'm not saying that's a bad thing, I'm just saying... (trails off) 

I'm still in business, I'm still making money. I mean, I had to lay low for a bit after... (pauses once more) but I've been careful. I've been careful, and I'm still out here. That's all that matters, right?"

crime: Criminal Mischief in the First Degree (severe property damage or vandalism)
story:
You want me to know about the vandalism thing, right? 

I mean, it was like four years ago. I was 31, I think. I don't really remember exactly. Anyway, it was a bunch of us, and we just did it, you know? We saw this building, and we thought it would be funny to spray paint it. I mean, it was a big building, but we still thought it was a good idea. I was the one who actually did it, though. I used my cousin's paint, and I'm pretty sure I'm the one who got the most coverage.

We were all just messing around, having a good time. I felt like I was in a movie or something. But then it hit me, like, afterwards, that it wasn't a good idea. I was scared, you know? My parents were gonna kill me. I mean, they already weren't speaking to me at the time. But this was gonna be it. I felt like an idiot.

We didn't even get that far before the cops showed up. They were like, all over us. I was trying to get away, and I slipped on some paint, and I fell. I think I sprained my ankle or something. Look, I'm not proud of it, but it was just something stupid we did. I got charged, and it was a real hassle. I had to do community service, and my parents were still mad at me for a while.

Honestly, I don't know why I'm telling you this. It's not like it's a big deal or anything anymore. It's just something I did. Can we just move on from it?

crime: Assault in the First Degree (serious physical injury with a deadly weapon)
story:
I mean, I know what I'm gettin' myself into, talkin' about this.  Assault in the first, that's the charge, right?  I'm 48 years old, been thinkin' about this a lot, tryin' to make sense of it. 

Look, I was in a fight, okay? I was scared, I felt like an idiot. I wasn't thinkin', just reactin'. There was this guy, he was bigger than me, and he was... I don't know, just takin' over. I mean, I'd had a few drinks, but that's not an excuse.

Honestly, I don't know what happened next. I just remember thinkin' I had to protect myself. I had a knife, and... (pauses) it just happened. He got hurt pretty bad, I think. Look, I'm not proud of it.

Two years ago, that's when it was. I was in therapy for a while, but it's not like it changed anything. I'm still thinkin' about it all the time. I mean, what if? You know, what if I'd just walked away? Would've been a lot better for everyone.

Anyway, I guess that's what I'm s'posed to be tellin' you. (pauses, then continues) I mean, I know I did it, and I'm willin' to face the consequences. Just, you know, tryin' to understand why."
